summary: Security re-review accepts the R2 fix. The OpenAI-compatible HTTP error path no longer reads or exposes provider response bodies and returns deterministic non-sensitive details instead.
decisions: Accepted the fix because non-OK HTTP responses now return code http_error, a generic status-bearing message, status, and details.providerError=true without reading the provider response body.

Accepted the regression coverage because the fake-fetch HTTP error test serializes the returned structured error and verifies sk-test-secret-do-not-leak, request material should stay private, and invalid_api_key are absent.
Limited this re-review to the previous security finding and direct regression risk from the R2 fix, as requested.

tests_run.result: Passed: R2 implementation handoff JSON is valid.
Passed: 11 tests across 4 suites, including the provider HTTP error body non-exposure regression.
Initial attempt compiled successfully but failed late during Next trace collection with ENOENT for .next\server\app\api\radar-tasks\[id]\route.js.nft.json.
Passed on sequential rerun: Next.js production build completed with the existing @next/swc-win32-x64-msvc invalid Win32 application warnings.

risks: Provider-specific HTTP error diagnostics are intentionally not surfaced through this client result; future debugging should use controlled server-side logging with explicit redaction if needed.
The first build attempt hit a transient-looking Next trace ENOENT after successful compilation, but the required build command passed on sequential rerun.
